value.content:
# Monte Carlo Portfolio Allocation – Optimization Overview

## Key Findings

- **Consistency Across Glidepath**: The optimal portfolio weights are stable and unchanged over the 10-year glidepath.
- **Allocation Emphasis**: The highest single weight is allocated to **US Treasuries (Cash Equivalents)** at ~14.2%.
- **Diversification**: All 17 asset classes are present in the portfolio, indicating a highly diversified structure. Most non-cash weights center around ~8% for major fixed income and alternatives, with smaller exposures in equities and select real assets.

---

## Optimal Asset Weights

| Asset                              | Weight   | Min Weight | Max Weight |
|-------------------------------------|----------|------------|------------|
| US Equity - Large Cap               | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| US Equity - Small/Mid Cap           | 0.86%    | 0.05%      | 49.90%     |
| Non-US Equity - Developed           | 3.52%    | 0.06%      | 49.91%     |
| Non-US Equity - Emerging            | 2.95%    | 0.09%      | 49.91%     |
| US Corporate Bonds - Core           | 2.81%    | 0.09%      | 49.91%     |
| US Corporate Bonds - Long Duration  | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| US Corporate Bonds - High Yield     | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| Non-US Debt - Developed             | 4.42%    | 0.07%      | 49.91%     |
| Non-US Debt - Emerging              | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| US Treasuries (Cash Equivalents)    | 14.22%   | 0.06%      | 99.92%     |
| TIPS (Inflation-Protected)          | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| Real Estate                         | 3.91%    | 0.06%      | 49.91%     |
| Hedge Funds                         | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| Commodities                         | 3.41%    | 0.05%      | 24.92%     |
| Infrastructure                      | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| Private Equity                      | 3.99%    | 0.06%      | 24.92%     |
| Private Debt                        | 3.99%    | 0.06%      | 24.92%     |

*Weights rounded to two decimals. Range reflects minimum and maximum weights explored in simulation.*

---

## Final Portfolio Structure

- **Fixed Income** (Core, Long Duration, High Yield, Non-US Developed/Emerging, Treasuries, TIPS): **~54.4%**
- **Equities** (US Large, US Small/Mid, Non-US Developed/Emerging): **~15.4%**
- **Alternatives** (Real Estate, Hedge Funds, Commodities, Infrastructure, Private Equity, Private Debt): **~17.2%**
- **Cash & Equivalents**: **14.2%**

---

## Notes

- **No Glidepath Change:** Asset weights do not change through years 1–10.
- **Risk Controls:** Minimum and maximum weights confirm the portfolio's broad allocation ranges per asset.
- **Applicability:** Portfolio is suitable for objectives requiring stable diversification and moderate risk stance.

---

**Simulation Package:** guardrails-mc v1.4  
**Run Hash:** 8b32c4c6f2b419aa

## Key Findings

- **Consistency Across Glidepath**: The optimal portfolio weights are stable and unchanged over the 10-year glidepath.
- **Allocation Emphasis**: The highest single weight is allocated to **US Treasuries (Cash Equivalents)** at ~14.2%.

weight.description: Weight for this asset (decimal between 0 and 1)
weight.title: Weight

GroupConstraint.description: Group constraint definition for portfolio optimization.

members.description: List of asset names in this group
members.title: Members

min.description: Minimum total weight for the group
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum total weight for the group
max.title: Max

GroupConstraint.title: GroupConstraint

schema.description:
Input for portfolio allocation using the Monte Carlo method.

This is an input for a Monte Carlo algorithm to find the optimal portfolio allocation
that meets specific risk and return objectives while respecting portfolio constraints.

portfolio_benchmark_weights.description: Optional custom weights for the benchmark portfolio. Provide a list of asset weights that must sum to 1.0. Example: [{'asset_name': 'US Stocks', 'weight': 0.6}, {'asset_name': 'Bonds', 'weight': 0.4}]. If not provided, will automatically generate equal weights for all assets.
portfolio_benchmark_weights.title: Portfolio Benchmark Weights

portfolio_box.description: Optional minimum and maximum weight constraints for each asset. Provide a list of box constraints. Example: [{'asset_name': 'US Stocks', 'min': 0.2, 'max': 0.7}, {'asset_name': 'Bonds', 'min': 0.1, 'max': 0.5}]. If not provided, will automatically generate constraints based on asset type: treasuries/cash get [0, 1.0], private equity/commodities get [0, 0.25], others get [0, 0.5].
portfolio_box.title: Portfolio Box

portfolio_groups.description: Optional group-level constraints for diversification. Each group has a name, 'members' (list of asset names), and optional 'max'/'min' (maximum/minimum total weight for the group). Example: [{'group_name': 'EquityMax', 'constraint': {'members': ['US Stocks', 'Intl Stocks'], 'max': 0.85}}] limits total equity to 85%. If not provided, will automatically create an equity group constraint (max 85%) if multiple equity assets exist.
portfolio_groups.title: Portfolio Groups

constraints_return_threshold.description: Optional minimum acceptable Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) as a decimal. Example: 0.05 means 5% annual return. Default is 0.02 (2%). Used to evaluate whether the generated returns meet or exceed this threshold with the specified probability.
constraints_return_threshold.title: Constraints Return Threshold

constraints_return_alpha.description: Optional probability level (between 0 and 1) for the return constraint. Example: 0.5 means the portfolio should meet the return threshold at least 50% of the time. Default is 0.5. Higher values make the constraint stricter.
constraints_return_alpha.title: Constraints Return Alpha

constraints_drawdown_threshold.description: Optional maximum acceptable drawdown (peak-to-trough decline) as a decimal. Example: 0.4 means maximum 40% loss from peak. Default is 0.6 (60%). The optimizer will try to ensure drawdowns stay below this threshold with the specified probability.
constraints_drawdown_threshold.title: Constraints Drawdown Threshold

constraints_drawdown_alpha.description: Optional probability level (between 0 and 1) for the drawdown constraint. Example: 0.7 means drawdowns should stay below the threshold at least 70% of the time. Default is 0.6. Higher values provide more downside protection.
constraints_drawdown_alpha.title: Constraints Drawdown Alpha

constraints_te_threshold.description: Optional maximum acceptable tracking error (deviation from benchmark) as a decimal. Example: 0.15 means maximum 15% deviation from the benchmark portfolio. Default is 0.20 (20%). Lower values keep the portfolio closer to the benchmark.
constraints_te_threshold.title: Constraints Te Threshold

constraints_te_alpha.description: Optional probability level (between 0 and 1) for the tracking error constraint. Example: 0.6 means tracking error should stay below the threshold at least 60% of the time. Default is 0.6. Higher values enforce tighter tracking to the benchmark.
constraints_te_alpha.title: Constraints Te Alpha

schema.title: PortfolioAllocationInput

*Weights rounded to two decimals. Range reflects minimum and maximum weights explored in simulation.*

---

## Final Portfolio Structure

- **Fixed Income** (Core, Long Duration, High Yield, Non-US Developed/Emerging, Treasuries, TIPS): **~54.4%**
- **Equities** (US Large, US Small/Mid, Non-US Developed/Emerging): **~15.4%**
- **Alternatives** (Real Estate, Hedge Funds, Commodities, Infrastructure, Private Equity, Private Debt): **~17.2%**
- **Cash & Equivalents**: **14.2%**

---

## Notes

- **No Glidepath Change:** Asset weights do not change through years 1–10.
- **Risk Controls:** Minimum and maximum weights confirm the portfolio's broad allocation ranges per asset.
- **Applicability:** Portfolio is suitable for objectives requiring stable diversification and moderate risk stance.

---

**Simulation Package:** guardrails-mc v1.4  
**Run Hash:** 8b32c4c6f2b419aa

inputs.title: Portfolio Allocation Report
inputs.executive_summary:
# Monte Carlo Portfolio Allocation – Optimization Overview

## Key Findings

- **Consistency Across Glidepath**: The optimal portfolio weights are stable and unchanged over the 10-year glidepath.
- **Allocation Emphasis**: The highest single weight is allocated to **US Treasuries (Cash Equivalents)** at ~14.2%.
- **Diversification**: All 17 asset classes are present in the portfolio, indicating a highly diversified structure. Most non-cash weights center around ~8% for major fixed income and alternatives, with smaller exposures in equities and select real assets.

---

## Optimal Asset Weights

| Asset                              | Weight   | Min Weight | Max Weight |
|-------------------------------------|----------|------------|------------|
| US Equity - Large Cap               | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| US Equity - Small/Mid Cap           | 0.86%    | 0.05%      | 49.90%     |
| Non-US Equity - Developed           | 3.52%    | 0.06%      | 49.91%     |
| Non-US Equity - Emerging            | 2.95%    | 0.09%      | 49.91%     |
| US Corporate Bonds - Core           | 2.81%    | 0.09%      | 49.91%     |
| US Corporate Bonds - Long Duration  | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| US Corporate Bonds - High Yield     | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| Non-US Debt - Developed             | 4.42%    | 0.07%      | 49.91%     |
| Non-US Debt - Emerging              | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| US Treasuries (Cash Equivalents)    | 14.22%   | 0.06%      | 99.92%     |
| TIPS (Inflation-Protected)          | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| Real Estate                         | 3.91%    | 0.06%      | 49.91%     |
| Hedge Funds                         | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| Commodities                         | 3.41%    | 0.05%      | 24.92%     |
| Infrastructure                      | 7.99%    | 0.06%      | 49.92%     |
| Private Equity                      | 3.99%    | 0.06%      | 24.92%     |
| Private Debt                        | 3.99%    | 0.06%      | 24.92%     |

*Weights rounded to two decimals. Range reflects minimum and maximum weights explored in simulation.*

---

## Final Portfolio Structure

- **Fixed Income** (Core, Long Duration, High Yield, Non-US Developed/Emerging, Treasuries, TIPS): **~54.4%**
- **Equities** (US Large, US Small/Mid, Non-US Developed/Emerging): **~15.4%**
- **Alternatives** (Real Estate, Hedge Funds, Commodities, Infrastructure, Private Equity, Private Debt): **~17.2%**
- **Cash & Equivalents**: **14.2%**

---
